iterations.title: Inventory + classify every doc; map link graph; list reconciliations and the README's honest disqualifiers
Resolve doc-refresh forks (F1-F9) and slice execution
E1: reconcile docs/ with reality - banners + assumption updates
E2: adversarial, humble root README - why-not-first, evidence-or-flag
E3: vault cross-links + ADOPTERS.md + MIT LICENSE + plugin/README alignment

plan.sketch: E1: reconcile docs/ with reality - banner vision.md + scope.md as historical design records; split constraints.md into current constraints vs historical decision-log; update questions.md assumptions to validated/partial/open each with evidence. DoD: every docs/ file is current or explicitly status-bannered; no claim reads as current that isn't.

E2: rewrite the root README as the adversarial, humble front door - opens with what lume is and why you should NOT use it; every claim [E]-cited or [U]-flagged; canonical install instructions; the iteration loop; a closing 'Status & evidence' section. DoD: leads with disqualifiers, no unevidenced/unflagged claim, install correct, humble tone.

E3: vault graph + artifacts - add relative-markdown cross-links across all docs rooted at README (no orphans); create ADOPTERS.md (table seeded with lume + PR process); add MIT LICENSE; align plugin/README tone and link it to the root install. DoD: opening the repo as an Obsidian vault yields one connected graph; ADOPTERS.md + LICENSE exist; plugin.json MIT claim now backed.

Closeout: retro (did the refresh buy its cost?), verify done-when (docs current/bannered, install findable, vault connected, README leads with why-not + evidence rule, ADOPTERS + LICENSE present), decisions logged, lume close.

workstream.title: Refresh the documentation to match the now-packaged plugin reality
